llm: LLM enabled with model gpt-5.5 from LLM_MODEL via OpenAI SDK.

candidate.name: pure_cublas_three_sgemm_qyh
candidate.strategy: Removes ATen matmul/add dispatch and explicit B.T transpose-copy while keeping all heavy work in cuBLAS; final SGEMM uses beta=1 to accumulate the rank-16 update directly into Y. Expected modest but real improvement over baseline, especially from eliminating intermediate add and transpose overhead.

candidate.name: pure_cublas_three_sgemm_qyh
candidate.strategy: Removes ATen matmul/add dispatch and explicit B.T transpose-copy while keeping all heavy work in cuBLAS; final SGEMM uses beta=1 to accumulate the rank-16 update directly into Y. Expected modest but real improvement over baseline, especially from eliminating intermediate add and transpose overhead.

avoid: Do not promote this baseline as an optimized implementation.
Do not write a custom CUDA kernel for the full W @ X GEMM; cuBLAS should handle the dominant term.
Do not materialize B.T as a separate transpose/copy.
Do not use fragile handwritten rank-16 kernels before validating the simpler cuBLAS low-rank SGEMM sequence.
Do not use opA=T/opB=T in SGEMM1 or SGEMM3 unless the full row-major/column-major mapping is re-derived and tested.
Do not treat U as column-major [16,d], do not use m=16,n=d for U, and do not use leading dimension 16 for the qyh-style U contract.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions beyond the runtime d and rank=16 contract.
Do not use result caching as an optimization unless the benchmark explicitly uses repeated identical tensor inputs as a valid workload.
evidence: Candidate is correct for all tested d values: 3584, 4096, and 4608 with max_abs_err=0 and rel_l2_err=0.
Aggregate speedup is effectively 1.0, matching the PyTorch/ATen baseline rather than improving it.
Per-shape latency is indistinguishable from torch baseline: 3584 is 3.724 ms vs 3.723 ms, 4096 is 5.819 ms vs 5.821 ms, 4608 is 7.452 ms vs 7.452 ms.
Profile identifies multiple ATen GEMM/add launches, so the implementation is still paying ATen dispatch/intermediate overhead and is not using a direct optimized cuBLAS sequence.
The dominant cost is the full d x d term W @ X, so the main term should remain a direct cuBLAS SGEMM rather than a custom CUDA GEMM.

recommended_actions: fuse rank-16 update into the output tensor
test scalar and vectorized rank-16 kernels
Reject this as an optimization candidate; it is only a correctness baseline.
Implement and validate a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path with no ATen matmul calls.
Use direct cuBLAS SGEMM for the main term W @ X; do not attempt a handwritten full GEMM.
Use the qyh-style mapping exactly: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W to produce Y = W @ X under the row-major-to-column-major cuBLAS mapping.
Avoid explicit B.T materialization. Compute the low-rank intermediate directly with SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T using A=X and B=B, with m=d, n=16, k=d, output U shape {d,16}, and ldc=d.
Accumulate the low-rank term into Y with SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N using A=U and B=A, with m=d, n=d, k=16, and beta=1 so the result becomes Y = W @ X + A @ (B.T @ X).
After implementing the pure cuBLAS path, validate correctness on all runtime d values before considering any rank-16 custom kernel alternatives.
If future variants show improvement for only one d while regressing another, use shape-aware dispatch or reject promotion.

suggested_fixes: If targeting the pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM strategy, replace ATen matmul calls with cuBLAS SGEMM calls using the current CUDA stream and include the required CUDA/cuBLAS headers.
Avoid materializing B.T in an optimized cuBLAS version by using cuBLAS transpose operation flags.
Optionally add TORCH_CHECK(d >= 3584 && d <= 4608) if the operator contract requires rejecting unsupported d values.
warnings: The implementation uses ATen matmul rather than a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM strategy; this is acceptable for an aten_reference candidate but does not satisfy pure cuBLAS/qyh-style optimization requirements.
The implementation explicitly materializes B.T via Bc.transpose(0, 1).contiguous(), which is unnecessary for a cuBLAS implementation and adds extra memory traffic.
The code validates shape consistency and rank-16 LoRA dimensions but does not explicitly enforce d in [3584, 4608]; it appears shape-general for square d x d inputs.

suggested_actions: fuse rank-16 update into the output tensor
test scalar and vectorized rank-16 kernels
